message:
fix(http): add LimitedWriter to prevent decompression bomb attacks

Introduce a LimitedWriter wrapper around Vec<u8> that enforces a strict
maximum byte count during decompression. The Gzip, Brotli, and Zstd
decompressors now write through LimitedWriter instead of directly into
an unbounded Vec, which means a malicious payload that decompresses to
an enormous size (e.g. a gzip bomb) will trigger an InvalidData error
before allocating unbounded memory.

The limit is set via the existing `max_size` field already present on
each decompressor — previously it was only checked after-the-fact on
the final output length. Now it's enforced incrementally on every write
call, so the allocator never sees the full decompressed size.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
